      <title>Luka Doncic Out For Regular Season With Grade 2 Hamstring Strain, Playoff Status Uncertain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284886/Luka-Doncic-Out-For-Regular-Season-With-Grade-2-Hamstring-Strain-Playoff-Status-Uncertain</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Lakers star Luka Doncic will miss the remainder of the regular season after an MRI Friday revealed a Grade 2 left hamstring strain. Doncic suffered the injury in the third quarter of Thursday's 139-96 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Dallas.</p>
<p>A Grade 2 strain typically requires approximately one month of recovery. The Lakers did not provide a timetable for his return beyond the final five regular-season games, leaving his availability for the playoffs in question.</p>
<p>The injury also pushes Doncic below the 65-game threshold required for end-of-season award eligibility. His agent, Bill Duffy of WME Basketball, told ESPN's Shams Charania that he will file an "Extraordinary Circumstances Challenge" on his client's behalf.</p>
<p>"This season, Luka Doncic has performed at a historic level, leading the league in scoring, carrying the Lakers to third place in the Western Conference and placing himself in the middle of one of the most tightly contested MVP races in memory," Duffy said in a statement. "To ensure that Luka's incredible accomplishments this season are rightly honored and he can be considered for the league's end-of-season awards, we intend to apply for an 'Extraordinary Circumstances Challenge' to the 65-game rule."</p>
<p>Duffy cited Doncic's early return from Slovenia following the December 4 birth of his second child as additional context for the filing. Doncic was back competing for the Lakers on December 6.</p>
<p>In 64 appearances this season, Doncic leads the NBA with 33.5 points per game and ranks third in assists (8.3), second among guards in rebounds (7.7) and sixth in steals (1.6). He finished <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284871/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Maintains-Sizable-NBA-MVP-Straw-Poll-Lead-Over-Victor-Wembanyama">a distant fourth in ESPN's final NBA MVP straw poll of 100 media members</a>, trailing Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama and Nikola Jokic.</p>
<p>Los Angeles holds the No. 3 seed in the Western Conference, one game ahead of Denver and 2.5 ahead of Houston, with tiebreakers secured against both clubs.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 19:20:37 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Investigating Bucks Over Giannis Antetokounmpo Availability Dispute</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284891/NBA-Investigating-Bucks-Over-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Availability-Dispute</link>
      <description><p>The NBA is investigating the Milwaukee Bucks over their handling of the player participation policy and potentially inconsistent statements regarding Giannis Antetokounmpo's health, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania on Friday. The league has already interviewed Antetokounmpo, the Bucks organization and team doctors.</p>
<p>At the center of the dispute is a direct contradiction as Antetokounmpo has told both the Bucks and the NBA he wants to play, but the team will not medically clear him. Milwaukee, meanwhile, has informed the league it does not believe Antetokounmpo is genuinely ready or willing to return, sources told Charania.</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo spoke directly to the conflict before Friday's game in Milwaukee.</p>
<p>"I'm available to play, but I'm not in the game," he told reporters. "I'm available to play today. Right now. I'm available."</p>
<p>He went further when addressing the organization's position.</p>
<p>"For somebody to come and tell me to not play or not to compete, it's like a slap in my face," Antetokounmpo said. "So, I don't know where the relationship goes from there."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo has missed 10 consecutive games with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284476/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Out-At-Least-One-Week-With-Bone-Bruise-In-Left-Knee">a hyperextended left knee and bone bruise</a> suffered March 15 against Indiana. Sources told Charania shortly after the injury that <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284511/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Refuses-Bucks-Request-To-Sit-Remainder-Of-Season">the Bucks wanted to shut him down for the remainder of the season and that he refused</a>. Doc Rivers said last week that Antetokounmpo had not even practiced, though team sources indicate he has been conducting increasingly intense pregame workouts before home games as a show of readiness.</p>
<p>The National Basketball Players Association issued a <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284657/NBPA-Critical-Of-Bucks-Injury-Management-Of-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">public rebuke of Milwaukee</a> late last month, with a statement implying the franchise is tanking and damaging the league's integrity.</p>
<p>The Bucks were <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284744/All-20-NBA-Playoff-Play-In-Teams-Determined-After-Bucks-Eliminated-In-Loss-to-Spurs">eliminated from playoff contention on March 28</a>, marking their first postseason absence since 2016. Antetokounmpo, 31, has appeared in just 36 games this season, by far the fewest of his career, dealing with two calf strains, an adductor strain and the knee hyperextension. Milwaukee has five regular-season games remaining and currently have <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">the 10th best lottery odds</a>.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284567/Bucks-Ownership-On-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Hell-Be-Extended-Or-Hell-Be-Traded">Antetokounmpo is expected to be traded by the Bucks this offseason</a> if he declines to commit to a five-year, $275 million extension he becomes eligible to sign in October.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 22:50:00 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284891/NBA-Investigating-Bucks-Over-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Availability-Dispute</guid>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Maintains Sizable NBA MVP Straw Poll Lead Over Victor Wembanyama</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284871/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Maintains-Sizable-NBA-MVP-Straw-Poll-Lead-Over-Victor-Wembanyama</link>
      <description><p>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander continues to lead the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type">NBA Most Valuable Player</a> straw poll with 88 first place votes ahead of Victor Wembanyama who has moved up into second place. Nikola Jokic, Luka Doncic and Jaylen Brown round out the top-5 in that order.</p>
<p>Wembanyama, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284848/Victor-Wembanyama-I-Do-Care-Deeply-About-Winning-NBA-MVP">who has been openly lobbying to win the award</a>, had 63 second place votes and eight first place votes while appearing on every ballot. Jokic received only three first place votes while Doncic received none in the poll. </p>
<p>Ten players received votes in the final MVP straw poll conducted by ESPN's Tim Bontemps and there were only two fourth place votes total for anyone outside the top-5 showing how solidified the top-4 is in the race.</p>
<p>Cade Cunningham was ruled ineligible for the voting with him now certain to not meet the 65-game minimum, while Anthony Edwards became ineligible after the voting. Doncic now sits at 64 games played, but suffered a hamstring injury in Thursday's loss by the Los Angele Lakers to the Oklahoma City Thunder.</p>
<p><span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Straw Poll Results</strong></span><br />1. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (88 first place, 9 second place, 3 third place votes)<br />2. Victor Wembanyama (8 first place, 63 second place, 19 third place, 9 fourth place, 1 fifth place vote)<br />3. Nikola Jokic (3 first place, 20 second place, 52 third place, 23 fourth place, 1 fifth place vote) <br />4. Luka Doncic (6 second place, 24 third place, 59 fourth place, 8 fifth place)<br />5. Jaylen Brown (1 first place, 2 second place, 2 third place, 7 fourth place, 68 fifth place votes)<br />6. Kawhi Leonard (1 fourth place votes, 15 fifth place votes)<br />7. Donovan Mitchell (1 fourth place, 5 fifth place votes) <br />8. Tyrese Maxey (1 fifth place vote)<br />8. Jalen Brunson (1 fifth place vote)<br />8. Anthony Edwards (1 fifth place vote)</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/ballot">Create your own shareable NBA awards ballot</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 07:07:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284871/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Maintains-Sizable-NBA-MVP-Straw-Poll-Lead-Over-Victor-Wembanyama</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Jason Kidd Refutes Mark Cuban's Claims On Luka Doncic Trade, Says He Was 'Informed At 11th Hour'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284882/Jason-Kidd-Refutes-Mark-Cubans-Claims-On-Luka-Doncic-Trade-Says-He-Was-Informed-At-11th-Hour</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Mavericks coach Jason Kidd directly disputed minority owner Mark Cuban's assertion that he played a role in the February 2025 trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers, stating Friday he was kept out of the process entirely until moments before the deal became public.</p>
<p>"Cuban has mentioned that I knew about the trade," Kidd said. "Unfortunately, as I have said, I was not part of the process. I was informed at the 11th hour. And that's the truth."</p>
<p>Kidd provided specific details on the sequence of events. Former general manager Nico Harrison summoned him to a room and told him a trade would go public at 11 p.m. News of the Doncic deal broke at 11:12 p.m. CT on February 1, 2025.</p>
<p>Cuban's accusation came earlier this week on the "Intersections" podcast, where the former majority owner said the trade that dismantled a contending roster fell on the coach and front office.</p>
<p>"That doesn't justify it for our coach and general manager to stand up and trade our best player," Cuban said.</p>
<p>Kidd said he called Cuban immediately after hearing the podcast comments.</p>
<p>"I called Mark right away," Kidd said. "We had that conversation. That's not a public conversation. But understanding that Mark, again, has done a lot for the Dallas Mavericks. And for the city. And for myself. This is not about 'He said, she said.' It's about his opinion. And I'm here to tell you the truth."</p>
<p>Dallas has struggled significantly since the trade. Harrison was fired in November amid a 3-8 start, and the franchise is now searching for a new head of basketball operations ahead of this year's draft. Doncic, meanwhile, is averaging 33.5 points per game and leads the NBA in scoring for the Lakers, who sit third in the Western Conference.</p>
<p>Kidd, entering his sixth season on the Dallas sideline, redirected focus toward rebuilding around rookie forward Cooper Flagg.</p>
<p>"We have planted our flag," Kidd said. "That's Cooper Flagg. We have an opportunity to build around him. It's an exciting time."</p>
<p>Cuban sold his majority stake to Miriam Adelson and Patrick Dumont in December 2023 for $3.5 billion. Kidd, who won a championship under Cuban as a player in 2011, was careful to separate their shared history from the current dispute.</p>
<p>"I played for Mark. Won a championship. He's one of the greatest owners I've played for," Kidd said. "Understanding roles have changed."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 15:05:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284882/Jason-Kidd-Refutes-Mark-Cubans-Claims-On-Luka-Doncic-Trade-Says-He-Was-Informed-At-11th-Hour</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kidd_Jason_dal_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kristaps Porzingis Keeping Options Open In Free Agency</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284887/Kristaps-Porzingis-Keeping-Options-Open-In-Free-Agency</link>
      <description><p>Kristaps Porzingis is keeping his options open this offseason despite finding comfort with the Golden State Warriors, declining to commit to a return when asked directly about his future with the franchise.</p>
<p>"It's hard to say," Porzingis told The Athletic after Thursday's 118-111 loss to Cleveland. "Of course, it would be nice for me to go ahead and say, 'Yes, I want to continue here,' and this and that. But the reality is I didn't have a good year at all. I barely showed what I'm capable of. And so I have to see what's out there."</p>
<p>The 30-year-old big man, acquired from the Atlanta Hawks before February's trade deadline, is averaging 17.5 points, 4.8 rebounds and 2.4 assists in 12 games with Golden State. He missed two weeks with an illness after debuting February 19, an absence he acknowledged was at least partially connected to his postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome diagnosis. Since returning March 7, he has remained in the lineup.</p>
<p>Porzingis is in the final year of a two-year, $60 million contract extension originally signed with the Boston Celtics. He has earned well over $200 million across his career.</p>
<p>One factor that could influence his decision is his relationship with Warriors vice president of player health Rick Celebrini, whom Porzingis has called the best in the business.</p>
<p>"I believe I'm in incredible hands here," Porzingis said. "When you have a really strong staff with somebody like me, maybe that's had some injuries throughout their career, to be in the best hands, it makes a difference for me to stay out there healthy."</p>
<p>Porzingis also cited his eagerness to finally share the floor with Stephen Curry, who is expected to return as early as Sunday against the Houston Rockets, as a factor worth considering.</p>
<p>"Who knows? Maybe we get out there and we can't play together," Porzingis said with a smile. "I don't know. Steph doesn't like to play with me - who knows? So obviously, I have to take that into account. But I think, with the experience that he has and, obviously, myself now at this point in my career, we will click."</p>
<p>Gui Santos expressed excitement about what the pairing could produce.</p>
<p>"You've got Porzingis, who can do whatever he wants in the paint. You've got Steph, who can do whatever he wants in the whole court," Santos said. "I don't know who can guard that."</p>
<p>Despite the uncertainty about his future, Porzingis has embraced his time in the Bay Area.</p>
<p>"Honestly, the team is great here," he said. "The team is great, the city is great, the organization is fantastic. The only thing is the time difference with Europe - it's 10 hours, it's too much. But everything else, I enjoy it here."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 19:32:53 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284887/Kristaps-Porzingis-Keeping-Options-Open-In-Free-Agency</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Porzingis_Kristaps_gsw_260306.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Kon Knueppel Leads NBA Rookie Of The Year Straw Poll Over Cooper Flagg</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284872/Kon-Knueppel-Leads-NBA-Rookie-Of-The-Year-Straw-Poll-Over-Cooper-Flagg</link>
      <description><p>Kon Knueppel leads Cooper Flagg in the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Rookie-of-the-Year/5">NBA Rookie of the Year </a>race by a comfortable margin in a straw poll conducted by ESPN's Tim Bontemps with 80 first place votes compared to 20 for the Dallas Mavericks franchise player.</p>
<p>Flagg entered the season as a comfortable Rookie of the Year favorite and largely met expectations, but Knueppel has had a historical season as the Charlotte Hornets have surged into the play-in picture. Knueppel is averaging 18.8 points, 5.4 rebounds and 3.4 assists while shooting 43.1 percent on 8.0 three-point attempts per game.</p>
<p>VJ Edgecombe of the Philadelphia 76ers is in third place in the poll.</p>

Derik Queen (1 third place vote)</p>

   <item>
      <title>South Carolina Ends UConn's Perfect Season, Auriemma-Staley Exchange Caps Heated Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284890/South-Carolina-Ends-UConns-Perfect-Season-Auriemma-Staley-Exchange-Caps-Heated-Game</link>
      <description><p>South Carolina exacted revenge on UConn on Friday, stunning the undefeated Huskies 62-48 in the Women's Final Four in Phoenix to advance to its third straight national championship game. The victory was punctuated by a heated postgame confrontation between coaches Geno Auriemma and Dawn Staley, after which Auriemma walked off the court without shaking hands with Staley or any South Carolina player or coach.</p>
<p>The Gamecocks had been reminded of last year's national championship loss to UConn every single morning since the offseason began. Sports performance coach Molly Binetti kept that final score displayed on weight room TVs by 6 a.m. daily, instilling the sting of defeat even in newcomers.</p>
<p>"We saw that in our faces, so even though I didn't experience that, I had to take on that because I knew that it was just more than just myself," said Ta'Niya Latson, who joined the program this season. "It was for the program, for the team and for the coaches."</p>
<p>South Carolina delivered on that motivation by dismantling an offense that had averaged 87 points per game this season. AP Player of the Year Sarah Strong was held to just 11 points. All-American Azzi Fudd managed eight points on 3-of-15 shooting. UConn shot just 31 percent from the field overall, ending its bid for a seventh undefeated championship season and finishing 38-1.</p>
<p>The Gamecocks didn't shoot much better at 37 percent, but their defensive physicality created problems throughout. South Carolina trailed by just two at halftime despite making only four shots in the second quarter.</p>
<p>The Gamecocks shot 46 percent from the field and converted eight of 10 free throws to build a 10-point lead with three minutes remaining in the third quarter. UConn responded with three consecutive three-pointers, but South Carolina answered with four straight points to take a five-point cushion into the fourth quarter, never looking back.</p>
<p>The frustration boiled over during a third-quarter timeout when Auriemma, already agitated, spoke directly to ESPN's Holly Rowe on the broadcast.</p>
<p>"There were six fouls called that quarter, all of them against us," Auriemma said. "And they've been beating the s- out of our guys down there the entire game. And I'm not making excuses because we haven't been able to make a shot. But this is ridiculous. Their coach ran some rage on the sideline and called the referees some names you don't want to hear. And now we get 6 to 0, and I got a kid with a ripped jersey. And they go, 'I didn't see it.' C'mon man, this is the national championship."</p>
<p>The ripped jersey belonged to Strong, who replaced her torn No. 21 with a blank No. 55 before returning to the floor. She was measured in her postgame explanation.</p>
<p>"It was an accident," Strong said. "I missed that shot. Ripped it by accident."</p>
<p>South Carolina finished 18-of-22 from the free-throw line. UConn converted just four of six attempts and shot 29 percent from three.</p>
<p>As the final seconds expired, Auriemma crossed the sideline toward Staley in what appeared to be a routine postgame exchange. Instead both coaches became visibly heated. Assistant coaches moved between them before Auriemma walked directly to the locker room, bypassing the handshake line entirely and leaving before his own players.</p>
<p>Staley told Rowe she was uncertain what prompted the confrontation.</p>
<p>"I have no idea," Staley said. "But imma let you know this: I'm of integrity. I'm of integrity. So if I did something wrong to Geno, I had no idea what I did. I guess he thought I didn't shake his hand at the beginning of the game. I didn't know, I went down there pregame, shook everybody on his staff's hand, I don't know what he came with after the game. But hey, sometimes things get heated, we move on."</p>
<p>The Gamecocks advance to Sunday's national championship game, extending one of the most dominant runs in women's college basketball history under Staley, who has now guided South Carolina to four title game appearances in five seasons.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 22:18:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284890/South-Carolina-Ends-UConns-Perfect-Season-Auriemma-Staley-Exchange-Caps-Heated-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_SouthCarolina.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>A'ja Wilson Confirms Return To Aces On 600 Percent Raise Supermax</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284884/Aja-Wilson-Confirms-Return-To-Aces-On-600-Percent-Raise-Supermax</link>
      <description><p>A'ja Wilson confirmed Friday she will return to the Las Vegas Aces for the 2026 WNBA season, ending any speculation about the league's best player testing free agency. Wilson made the announcement at USA Basketball training camp.</p>
<p>"I love Vegas, I'm not leaving Vegas," Wilson said. "I'm not looking anywhere, I'm looking to win another one, I'm looking to defend a championship that we have in Las Vegas."</p>
<p>Wilson, an unrestricted free agent, is expected to sign a supermax contract worth $1.4 million this season under the new collective bargaining agreement, per the Las Vegas Review-Journal. She earned $200,000 in 2025.</p>
<p>The 28-year-old has won three championships, four MVP awards, three Defensive Player of the Year trophies and two WNBA Finals MVP honors across eight seasons with Las Vegas. She led the league in scoring last season at 23.4 points per game and added 10.2 rebounds per game.</p>
<p>Wilson's return anchors a roster that will require significant reconstruction. The Aces currently have just one player under contract and must re-sign Chelsea Gray, Jackie Young and other key contributors from their title-winning core. The franchise also faces potential losses in Friday's WNBA expansion draft, where it could forfeit rights to up to two players.</p>
<p>Las Vegas retained its full coaching staff and added former Dallas Wings assistant Nola Henry to the bench this offseason.</p>
<p>Wilson is also competing with Team USA this week as the national team pursues a fifth consecutive World Cup gold medal. She was part of the last two gold-medal-winning squads, including a standout 2022 run in which she joined the team midway through the tournament after delivering an Aces championship.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 15:21:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284884/Aja-Wilson-Confirms-Return-To-Aces-On-600-Percent-Raise-Supermax</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Aja_lva_240522.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Execs Increasingly View 2026 NBA Draft As Deep But Uncertain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284874/Execs-Increasingly-View-2026-NBA-Draft-As-Deep-But-Uncertain</link>
      <description><p>The 2026 NBA Draft features a deep and gifted pool of prospects, but evaluators across the league are grappling with a sobering reality that nobody knows which players will actually be the best. With AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer all done with their freshman seasons, the race for the top pick remains genuinely unsettled. Beyond that trio are high upside players such as Caleb Wilson, Kingston Flemings, Keaton Wagler, Darius Acuff Jr. and Mikel Brown Jr.</p>
<p>This runs counter to highly anticipated drafts with a clear No. 1 such as Victor Wembanyama in 2023 and Cooper Flagg in 2025.</p>
<p>"You have high confidence that at least some of them are going to be really, really good. You're not really sure which ones," a Western Conference executive told ESPN. "You don't want to be drafting at 1 and end up with the fifth-best player."</p>
<p>Among scouts and executives surveyed, Dybantsa has emerged as the slight favorite for the top selection, with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">the lottery next month</a> expected to play a significant role in how teams approach the board.</p>
<p>"I guess you'd rather fail with [AJ] and his upside, than not," a Western Conference general manager said. "I just think that [AJ], because he's 6-9 and he could be like 6-10, 230 [pounds] by the time he's 25 years old, he could just be a monster. I think you've just got to go down swinging with him if you go down."</p>
<p>Peterson retains firm support despite an uneven Kansas freshman campaign that featured both injury absences and stretches of limited involvement alongside undeniable flashes of elite ability.</p>
<p>"I think Peterson is the most talented guy," an Eastern Conference executive said, "but the injury stuff is a real question. That's a valid concern, and it's been a weird year. But he's a huge talent, and he was No. 1 at the start of the season for a reason."</p>
<p>Boozer rounds out the top tier after one of the most statistically impressive freshman seasons in recent college basketball history. The Duke forward is the only player nationally to finish in the top 12 in both scoring (22.5 points per game) and rebounding (10.2), guiding the Blue Devils to within a half-court miracle of the Final Four.</p>
<p>One Western Conference executive compared the class's uncertainty to the 2024 draft headlined by Zaccharie Risacher, but with considerably higher upside across the board.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 10:55:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284874/Execs-Increasingly-View-2026-NBA-Draft-As-Deep-But-Uncertain</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tommy Lloyd Agrees To New Five-Year Deal With Arizona</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284879/Tommy-Lloyd-Agrees-To-New-Five-Year-Deal-With-Arizona</link>
      <description><p>Headline Options:</p>
<p>Tommy Lloyd Signs New Five-Year Deal With Arizona, Spurns North Carolina Interest<br />Tommy Lloyd Commits To Arizona With New Deal Worth $7.5M Annually: "I'm Staying"<br /> <br />Tommy Lloyd ended days of uncertainty Friday, announcing he has signed a new five-year contract with Arizona and will not pursue the North Carolina head coaching vacancy. Lloyd will average $7.5 million annually over the life of the fully guaranteed deal, starting at nearly $7.2 million in 2026-27, making him one of the five highest-paid coaches in college basketball, per ESPN sources.</p>
<p>"I'm staying at Arizona," Lloyd said at a Friday news conference. "Arizona basketball, you guys know what it means to me, and when I say it's a special place, that always comes from the bottom of my heart."</p>
<p>Lloyd addressed the persistent speculation about Michael Jordan personally recruiting him to Chapel Hill, calling the rumored phone calls fiction, before delivering a pointed declaration of loyalty.</p>
<p>"I made a decision that my Michael Jordan is Steve Kerr, and I'm proud to be an Arizona Wildcat," he said.</p>
<p>The agreement includes significant performance bonuses and an expanded staff salary pool. The buyout to leave for another program, which stood at $9 million under his previous contract, is expected to remain substantial in both directions.</p>
<p>Lloyd's commitment arrives at the peak of his tenure in Tucson. He is 148-35 in five seasons, the most wins by any head coach in NCAA history through their first five seasons. Arizona swept the Big 12 regular-season and conference tournament titles this year and advanced to the program's first Final Four since 2001, where the Wildcats face Michigan on Saturday.</p>
<p>Athletic director Desiree Reed-Francois was direct in her assessment. "Tommy Lloyd is the best coach in college basketball, and we have a strong conviction in the future of Arizona Basketball under his leadership," she said in a statement.</p>
<p>Senior guard Jaden Bradley echoed that sentiment from the locker room. "Excited that he signed back with Arizona," Bradley said. "I know the fan base is excited as well."</p>
<p>Since arriving from Gonzaga in 2021, where he spent two decades as Mark Few's top assistant, Lloyd has guided Arizona to three conference regular-season titles and four Sweet 16 appearances in five NCAA tournaments.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 13:58:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284879/Tommy-Lloyd-Agrees-To-New-Five-Year-Deal-With-Arizona</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lloyd_Tommy_az_260316.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Warriors Had Framework Of Kawhi Leonard Trade Done Before Clippers Pulled Out</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284873/Warriors-Had-Framework-Of-Kawhi-Leonard-Trade-Done-Before-Clippers-Pulled-Out</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors came close to acquiring Kawhi Leonard at the February trade deadline, with much of the deal's framework in place before the Los Angeles Clippers withdrew from negotiations, multiple league sources told The Athletic this week. The Warriors also pursued a trade with the Milwaukee Bucks for Giannis Antetokounmpo at the deadline.</p>
<p>The near-miss on Leonard underscores Golden State's aggressive push to pair a second star alongside Stephen Curry. Leonard's name continues to surface in discussions about potential Warriors moves, either through renewed trade talks or, as multiple sources around the league have noted, a possible contract voiding stemming from the NBA's ongoing salary cap circumvention investigation into the Clippers.</p>
<p>The Warriors are expected to remain active in their pursuit of a high-profile addition this offseason. Sources indicate the franchise intends to chase at least one more star to complete a projected starting lineup that would also include Draymond Green, Jimmy Butler and potentially Kristaps Porzingis.</p>
<p>Golden State enters the offseason with significant decisions ahead. Curry is nearing a return from injury, Steve Kerr's coaching future remains unresolved, and the front office under Mike Dunleavy is operating with clear championship ambitions rather than any inclination toward a full roster reset.</p>
<p>Owner Joe Lacob, Dunleavy and Curry are all said to want Kerr back for at least one more season. No formal contract extension discussions have taken place, and Kerr has not yet indicated publicly whether he intends to return.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 10:41:48 EDT</pubDate>

   <item>
      <title>USC's JuJu Watkins Back On Court At USA Basketball Camp, Expected Ready For Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284889/USCs-JuJu-Watkins-Back-On-Court-At-USA-Basketball-Camp-Expected-Ready-For-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>JuJu Watkins is making steady progress in her return from a torn right ACL, shooting on the practice court at the Phoenix Mercury facility during USA Basketball national team training camp without a knee brace. The Southern California star, who lost her entire 2025-26 season to the injury, expects to be ready for the start of next college season.</p>
<p>"Recovery's coming along very well," Watkins said. "Just staying with it, and I'll be back soon. Getting closer to the finish line, so I'm grateful about that."</p>
<p>Watkins tore her ACL in the second round of the 2025 NCAA Tournament against Mississippi State on March 24, leaving her more than a year removed from the injury. Her participation at the current Phoenix camp marks an upgrade from December's training camp at Duke, where she watched entirely from the sidelines.</p>
<p>"I think this journey has definitely taught me to have a lot of patience," Watkins said. "So many ups and downs within the process, but just staying true to myself and staying true to the process."</p>
<p>Team USA managing director Sue Bird offered an encouraging assessment of Watkins' trajectory.</p>
<p>"JuJu's in a great spot," Bird said. "Just in talking to her, you can see she's in a really settled place, a really calm place. Ready to get back on the court. As you get closer to the end, it actually gets harder because you know you're ready, but you still have to take your time with it. But she looks great."</p>
<p>Watkins earned AP Player of the Year honors in 2024-25 and was a two-time All-American in her first two seasons. The No. 1 recruit in the 2023 class, she watched USC finish 18-14 and fall to South Carolina in the second round of this year's NCAA Tournament without her.</p>
<p>"A lot of ways we can improve. A lot of things we do well," Watkins said of observing the Trojans from the sideline. "Just being able to see things from a different perspective has definitely helped and given me some things I can continue to contribute to the team next year."</p>
<p>USC enters next season with the nation's top recruiting class, headlined by No. 1 recruit Saniyah Hall. Coach Lindsay Gottlieb has landed the top-ranked player in the country in three of the past four recruiting cycles.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 21:24:12 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284889/USCs-JuJu-Watkins-Back-On-Court-At-USA-Basketball-Camp-Expected-Ready-For-Next-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Watkins_Juju_usc_240402.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>UConn's Sarah Strong Named AP Women's Basketball Player Of The Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284888/UConns-Sarah-Strong-Named-AP-Womens-Basketball-Player-Of-The-Year</link>
      <description><p>Sarah Strong was named the Associated Press women's basketball Player of the Year Thursday, becoming just the fifth sophomore in history to claim the honor after guiding UConn to an undefeated 38-0 record and a Final Four berth. Strong received 25 of 31 votes from the national media panel, with Vanderbilt's Mikayla Blakes earning four votes and UCLA's Lauren Betts collecting the remaining two.</p>
<p>Strong joins an elite group of sophomores to win the award since its 1995 inception, including Oklahoma's Courtney Paris (2007), UConn's Maya Moore (2009) and Breanna Stewart (2014), and USC's JuJu Watkins, who claimed the honor last season. It is the 13th time a Huskies player has won, with Paige Bueckers being the last before Strong in 2021.</p>
<p>"Anybody that's watched us play would probably tell you that she's the heart and soul of our team," UConn coach Geno Auriemma said. "She elevates the play of everyone on our team."</p>
<p>Auriemma, who has coached Breanna Stewart, Sue Bird, Maya Moore and Diana Taurasi, placed Strong firmly in that company.</p>
<p>"The great ones, the great ones all had it. I never saw Taurasi nervous," he said. "You know that they came in as freshmen and you can tell by the look in their eyes, 'I can handle this.' She's ready for that one."</p>
<p>Auriemma described Strong's defining quality as an uncommon composure.</p>
<p>"You don't see that look in her eyes, you know, like, oh, my God, there's always just a calmness," Auriemma said. "That allows her to just be free and fluid and play without worry."</p>
<p>Strong is averaging 18.6 points, 7.6 rebounds, 3.4 steals and 1.6 blocks per game in just 27 minutes of action. She is shooting 59.4 percent from the field, 40.4 percent from three and 84.8 percent from the free-throw line. Against top-25 opponents, she elevated further, averaging over 20 points and 10 rebounds per game.</p>
<p>Strong reached 1,000 career points in her 59th game, the third-fastest player in program history to hit the mark. She also earned Big East Player of the Year honors and was named Most Outstanding Player of the Fort Worth Regional.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 21:22:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284888/UConns-Sarah-Strong-Named-AP-Womens-Basketball-Player-Of-The-Year</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Connecticut.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Cameron Boozer Becomes Fifth Freshman To Win AP Player Of The Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284885/Cameron-Boozer-Becomes-Fifth-Freshman-To-Win-AP-Player-Of-The-Year</link>
      <description><p>Cameron Boozer has been named <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/ncaa/awards/3/Associated-Press-National-Awards-Honors">the Associated Press men's college basketball Player of the Year</a>, becoming only the fifth freshman to win the award and the second straight Duke player to claim the honor following Cooper Flagg last season. Boozer received 59 of 61 votes from AP Top 25 voters, with BYU's AJ Dybantsa collecting the remaining two.</p>
<p>Boozer joins fellow Blue Devils star Cooper Flagg last year, another Duke player in Zion Williamson (2019), Kentucky's Anthony Davis (2012) and Texas' Kevin Durant (2007) as freshmen to win the AP award. Each went No. 1 or No. 2 in the NBA draft that year.</p>
<p>"It just goes to show more about what our team has done, just because I think that really helps awards like this, having great team success," Boozer told the AP. "It's really just not me."</p>
<p>The 6-foot-9, 250-pound forward averaged 22.5 points and 10.2 rebounds per game this season while shooting 55.6 percent overall and 39.1 percent from three. He finished tied for the national lead with 22 double-doubles and added 4.1 assists per game, helping Duke reach No. 1 in the AP Top 25 and earn the top overall NCAA Tournament seed before falling in the Elite Eight.</p>
<p>Boozer joins a distinguished group of freshmen AP honorees that includes Flagg, Zion Williamson, Anthony Davis and Kevin Durant. Each of those players was selected No. 1 or No. 2 in the NBA draft that year.</p>
<p>"I'm very grateful just that I'm even in those conversations," Boozer said. "Sometimes you've got to take a step back and just remember that once upon a time, you were a kid dreaming to be here."</p>
<p>Duke associate head coach Chris Carrawell placed Boozer in elite company. "We've been fortunate enough the last two years to have two of the best freshmen to ever play in college basketball back to back," Carrawell said. "And Cam is right up there."</p>
<p>Michigan coach Dusty May offered perhaps the most precise assessment of Boozer's impact. "His desire to rebound the ball, to set physical screens, to play to his advantages, is as impressive as any freshman that I can recall," May said.</p>
<p>Boozer is the ninth different Duke player to win the AP award, giving the Blue Devils more winners than any program in the country. UCLA is the next closest with five.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 15:30:28 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284885/Cameron-Boozer-Becomes-Fifth-Freshman-To-Win-AP-Player-Of-The-Year</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Boozer_Cameron_duke_251105.jpg" />
   </item>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284883/Mac-McClung-Wins-G-League-MVP-For-2nd-Time-In-Career</link>
      <description><p>Mac McClung has been named <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/gleague/awards/by-type">G League Most Valuable Player</a> for the second time in his career. McClung previously won the award in 2024.</p>
<p>McClung averaged a league-high 31.8 points, 7.9 assists and 3.6 rebounds, while shooting 51.5 percent from the field and 38.1 percent from three-point range in 40 games for the Windy City Bulls.</p>
<p>Houston Rockets two-way guard Tristen Newton (Rio Grande Valley Vipers) and Stockton Kings guard-forward DaQuan Jeffries finished second and third.</p>
<p>The Bulls converted McClung to a Two-Way contract in February.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Nebraska's Fred Hoiberg Named AP Men's Basketball Coach Of The Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284881/Nebraskas-Fred-Hoiberg-Named-AP-Mens-Basketball-Coach-Of-The-Year</link>
      <description><p>Fred Hoiberg was named the Associated Press men's basketball coach of the year Friday after guiding Nebraska to a 28-win season, the program's first NCAA Tournament victory and a Sweet 16 appearance. Hoiberg received 17 votes from a 61-person media panel, edging Duke's Jon Scheyer (13 votes) to become the Big Ten's first national coach of the year since Michigan's Juwan Howard in 2021. Arizona's Tommy Lloyd received 11 votes.</p>
<p>"It took us some time to get here, but it was all about getting the right players in here, especially the ones that the fans could get behind," Hoiberg said.</p>
<p>The Cornhuskers set a school record with 15 Big Ten wins and matched the program record of 26 victories before March Madness even began. Nebraska posted its first tournament win in nine all-time tries with a 76-47 victory over Troy before falling to Iowa in the Sweet 16.</p>
<p>"When the sting does wear off, which it will at some point, maybe, these guys deserve a lot of credit for what they have done for Nebraska basketball," Hoiberg said.</p>
<p>The turnaround carries deep personal significance. Hoiberg was born in Lincoln, his parents are Nebraska graduates, his maternal grandfather Jerry Bush coached the Cornhuskers from 1953 to 1963, and his paternal grandfather Otto Hoiberg taught at the school for 30 years.</p>
<p>When Hoiberg took over in 2019, Nebraska had reached the tournament once in 21 seasons. His first two years produced a combined 7-45 record before the program found its footing. Led by Iowa transfer Pryce Sandfort, Dutch big man Rienk Mast and senior point guard Sam Hoiberg, Fred's son, Nebraska won its first 20 games and reached No. 5 in the AP Top 25, the program's highest-ever ranking.</p>
<p>Scheyer finished runner-up after Duke claimed the No. 1 ranking for four straight weeks and earned a No. 1 NCAA Tournament seed. He adjusted his approach from last year's Final Four run, building around star freshman Cameron Boozer's interior dominance.</p>
<p>Lloyd, the 2022 AP coach of the year in his first Arizona season, guided the Wildcats to nine weeks at No. 1 and their first Final Four since 2001.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 15:00:04 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284881/Nebraskas-Fred-Hoiberg-Named-AP-Mens-Basketball-Coach-Of-The-Year</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Hoiberg_Fred_chi_180103.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>UConn's Coach Dan Hurley Won't Shy Away From Sideline Behavior</title>

      <description><p>Dan Hurley believes the backlash about his on-court behavior is fair.</p>
<p>Just over 24 hours before UConn's matchup against Illinois in the Final Four, Hurley said he is not looking for any sympathy regarding the perception of his coaching style.</p>
<p>"I'm not a victim. I've done everything. I did what I did," he said during his pregame news conference Friday. "We don't allow victims in our program, and I'm not a 53-year-old man sitting up here like I'm some victim. I don't want to waste a lot of time with it because it takes away from the team. But for me, the way I view what we're going into, in the game, when some people, again, view it as a game, just my family, how I was raised in the sport, where I'm from in Jersey, we look at it more like a battle."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Keaton Wagler, Darius Acuff, Kingston Flemings Competing For No. 5 In 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284878/Keaton-Wagler-Darius-Acuff-Kingston-Flemings-Competing-For-No-5-In-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>With AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer and Caleb Wilson forming a clear top tier, the next wave of NBA Draft prospects is dominated by a cluster of ballhandling guards competing for the No. 5 spot. Arkansas' Darius Acuff Jr., Houston's Kingston Flemings and Illinois' Keaton Wagler lead that group, with Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr. and Arizona's Brayden Burries also drawing support depending on the evaluator.</p>
<p>Combine measurements are expected to play a significant role in separating the group. Acuff (listed at 6-3) and Flemings (6-4) have drawn particular attention regarding size, while Wagler (6-6) carries a natural advantage at the position.</p>
<p>Acuff's explosive scoring and shotmaking down the stretch, including a dominant SEC Tournament run, converted numerous NBA evaluators. His defensive limitations, however, remain a polarizing topic.</p>
<p>"Just the shotmaking, killer instinct he has shown, his ability to score at all three levels," an Eastern Conference executive said. "His defense does worry me some, but he has had to carry a pretty large load."</p>
<p>The skepticism on Acuff runs deeper for some.</p>
<p>"I think there's never been a worse time to build around bad defensive guards," a Western Conference executive said. "It ends up being Derrick White and Jrue Holiday and Alex Caruso, go down the list. Trae Young was traded for basically nothing. People fall in love with guards, and I know of a bunch of teams who are falling in love with Acuff right now."</p>
<p>Flemings earned strong reviews for his two-way play and willingness to thrive under demanding Houston coach Kelvin Sampson, known for his rigorous standards.</p>
<p>"Kingston operates a little bit differently - a better overall package when you look at both ends of the floor," a Western Conference executive said. "He's really impressive to come in as a freshman and play the role he's played on one of the top teams in the country."</p>
<p>His shot mechanics remain an area of focus, though at least one evaluator is willing to overlook the concern.</p>
<p>"It's so impressive that the big shots are his, on a team with experienced guards that have been there and won at that school," an Eastern Conference scout said. "You see his shot form, but I'll bet on all the other stuff and we can work with his jump shot."</p>
<p>Wagler's rise is perhaps the draft's most striking development. A three-star high school recruit with no national profile entering the season, the Illinois guard transformed himself into a projected mid-lottery pick during the Illini's Final Four run.</p>
<p>"I'll take my chances on a 6-6 guy who can basically run the show," a Western Conference executive said. "And he plays with such tremendous pace and feel."</p>
<p>His mental makeup drew equal praise from another evaluator.</p>
<p>"The thing that unites all of the top players is being elite mentally, and that's the thing that gets underrated in all of this," an Eastern Conference executive said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 11:31:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284878/Keaton-Wagler-Darius-Acuff-Kingston-Flemings-Competing-For-No-5-In-2026-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wagler_Keaton_il_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Caleb Wilson Could Vault Into Top Three Despite Injury-Plagued Freshman Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284877/Caleb-Wilson-Could-Vault-Into-Top-Three-Despite-Injury-Plagued-Freshman-Season</link>
      <description><p>North Carolina freshman Caleb Wilson entered the season as the No. 4 prospect in ESPN's top 100 rankings and took a major developmental leap before injuries derailed his momentum. Despite breaking his left hand in February and his right thumb in practice while working toward a postseason return, Wilson has quietly built a strong case for a top-three selection.</p>
<p>"I think he could easily go three," an Eastern Conference scout said. "I could see him being preferred to Boozer there."</p>
<p>The physical, versatile forward has drawn comparisons to Pascal Siakam and Aaron Gordon for his combination of length, athleticism, defensive intensity and secondary playmaking ability. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/prospects/stats/Advanced_Stats/All/">Wilson ranked second among all draft prospects in player efficiency rating during the 2025-26 season</a>, trailing only Cameron Boozer.</p>
<p>Multiple scouts and executives believe his upside could separate him from the pack between now and late June, though his candidacy is viewed as heavily dependent on lottery results and team fit.</p>
<p>"The intel piece on him is incredible," an Eastern Conference executive said.</p>
<p>Wilson's supporters draw direct comparisons to established NBA talent at the next level.</p>
<p>"Caleb has been more productive and played harder in college than Jalen Johnson, who in college was more perimeter oriented," an Eastern Conference executive said. "If you thought Cam [Boozer] is Al Horford or something like that, and you thought Caleb was like Jalen, then you could go Caleb."</p>
<p>Wilson's draft stock is viewed as one of the more fluid situations at the top of the board heading into the predraft process.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 11:22:52 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284877/Caleb-Wilson-Could-Vault-Into-Top-Three-Despite-Injury-Plagued-Freshman-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Caleb_unc_251223.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Scouts Agree Cameron Boozer Has Highest Floor In Draft, Mixed Opinion On Upside</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284876/Scouts-Agree-Cameron-Boozer-Has-Highest-Floor-In-Draft-Mixed-Opinion-On-Upside</link>
      <description><p>Cameron Boozer enters the NBA Draft as the prospect with the most universally acknowledged floor in the entire class, yet conflicting opinions about his athleticism and positional versatility have created one of the more polarizing draft profiles in recent memory. Virtually every executive and scout interviewed agreed on his baseline value,  but consensus breaks down sharply when the conversation turns to his ceiling.</p>
<p>"I think people have gone numb to him and the production and the archetype," an Eastern Conference executive said. "The versatility he brings offensively is 1-of-1 in this class. I understand the problems: the finishing, lack of rim protection, heavy legs. But he is an offensive catalyst to me - Sabonis, Sengun, any of the others who have been knocked for athleticism. IQ and feel have overcome so much. I think he has answered every question he can."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/prospects/stats/Advanced_Stats/All/">Boozer led all NBA draft prospects in player efficiency rating during the 2025-26 NCAA season</a> ahead of Caleb Wilson, capping a dominant run at Duke that included an Elite Eight appearance. The son of former All-Star power forward Carlos Boozer has won at every level he has played.</p>
<p>Skeptics, however, question whether his lack of explosiveness and quickness limits him to a single position.</p>
<p>"I'm not a huge fan of a one-position 4," an Eastern Conference scout said. "If that's what he is, then you have to build your team specifically around that, given his lack of versatility."</p>
<p>One Eastern Conference executive pushed back on that framing, drawing a comparison to another prospect who faced similar skepticism before his NBA career.</p>
<p>"He doesn't look the part athletically, so people put limits on him," the executive said of Boozer, referencing parallels to Luka Doncic. "I don't know why people keep putting a ceiling on him before he's 19. There's a lot of ways to talk about athleticism, not just how high you can jump, and he always plays the most efficient way he can to help his team win."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 11:16:05 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284876/Scouts-Agree-Cameron-Boozer-Has-Highest-Floor-In-Draft-Mixed-Opinion-On-Upside</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Boozer_Cameron_duke_251105.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Darryn Peterson Continues To Be Most Talented, Most Complex Player At Top Of 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284875/Darryn-Peterson-Continues-To-Be-Most-Talented-Most-Complex-Player-At-Top-Of-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Darryn Peterson remains in contention for the top pick in the NBA Draft, but persistent leg injuries and cramping issues that limited him to 24 games this season have triggered heightened scrutiny from front offices across the league. Gathering medical clarity on Peterson has become one of the most critical steps in the predraft process.</p>
<p>"It's definitely a thing," an Eastern Conference scout said. "If you're a team that's in the mix for the No. 1 or 2 pick, you're going to have to get a ton of information on what happened, and why."</p>
<p>"I think it forces you to start asking a bunch of questions that you normally wouldn't have to ask for a guy like [Peterson]," a Western Conference executive said. "Just the management of his situation and his health exposed him to some level of further investigation, past the already intense investigation a No. 1 pick would go under. In the coming months, there is a non-zero chance that the root of this issue, depending on what it is, could affect his stock."</p>
<p>When healthy, Peterson's case for the top selection is compelling. He thoroughly outplayed AJ Dybantsa in the first half of their head-to-head matchup in Lawrence, Kansas, in front of dozens of NBA executives, flashing the kind of scoring ability that made him the consensus No. 1 prospect entering the season.</p>
<p>"I think Peterson is the most talented guy," an Eastern Conference executive said, "but the injury stuff is a real question. That's a valid concern, and it's been a weird year. But he's a huge talent, and he was No. 1 at the start of the season for a reason."</p>
<p>Despite the concerns, Dybantsa has solidified his standing as the current frontrunner among scouts and executives surveyed, with next month's lottery expected to heavily influence how teams approach the top of the board.</p>
<p>"I guess you'd rather fail with [AJ] and his upside, than not," a Western Conference general manager said. "I just think that [AJ], because he's 6-9 and he could be like 6-10, 230 [pounds] by the time he's 25 years old, he could just be a monster. I think you've just got to go down swinging with him if you go down."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 03 Apr 2026 11:12:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284875/Darryn-Peterson-Continues-To-Be-Most-Talented-Most-Complex-Player-At-Top-Of-2026-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Peterson_Darryn_ku_251222.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Kansas Center Flory Bidunga To Declare For NBA Draft, Enter Transfer Portal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284866/Kansas-Center-Flory-Bidunga-To-Declare-For-NBA-Draft-Enter-Transfer-Portal</link>
      <description><p>Kansas sophomore center Flory Bidunga intends to declare for the NBA Draft while maintaining his college eligibility, sources told ESPN Thursday. Bidunga will also enter the NCAA transfer portal, preserving his option to return to college basketball.</p>
<p>Bidunga would immediately rank as the top available player among the more than 800 athletes who have declared for the portal so far, according to ESPN's Jeff Borzello. The portal formally opens Tuesday, the day after the NCAA championship game.</p>
<p>On the professional side, ESPN's Jeremy Woo projects Bidunga as a second-round pick at No. 44 in the current draft class.</p>
<p>The 6-foot-10, 235-pound big man averaged 13.3 points and 9.0 rebounds per game for the Jayhawks this season, finishing third in the Big 12 in rebounding. He led the conference in both blocks (2.6 per game) and field goal percentage, connecting on 64 percent of his attempts, nearly seven points above the next closest qualifier.</p>
<p>Bidunga was also a candidate for national defensive player of the year honors, underlining his two-way impact at the collegiate level.</p>
<p>Should he opt to remain in college for his junior season, Bidunga figures to be among the most coveted transfer targets in the country. Interior size and production command a significant premium on the current transfer market, and few players in the portal can match his combination of rim protection and finishing ability.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 22:26:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284866/Kansas-Center-Flory-Bidunga-To-Declare-For-NBA-Draft-Enter-Transfer-Portal</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Kansas_1.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Arizona's Tommy Lloyd: 'I'm Not A Multitasker' Amid North Carolina Head Coach Rumors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284865/Arizonas-Tommy-Lloyd-Im-Not-A-Multitasker-Amid-North-Carolina-Head-Coach-Rumors</link>
      <description><p>Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd declined to engage with speculation linking him to the North Carolina vacancy Thursday, saying his complete attention belongs to the Wildcats ahead of Saturday's Final Four matchup against Michigan.</p>
<p>"I'm a simple guy. I'm kind of just one thing at a time. I'm not a multitasker," Lloyd said at his pregame news conference. "You can ask my wife. So, I'm 100% locked in on Arizona basketball right now, and I'm excited to see what this team can do. I have a real strong belief in this team and this team deserves my full attention, so that's what I'm giving."</p>
<p>North Carolina fired Hubert Davis last month, creating one of college basketball's premier vacancies. Lloyd, Chicago Bulls coach Billy Donovan and Michigan's Dusty May have all been identified as top candidates. The fact that UNC has yet to name a replacement has fueled speculation that its primary target remains active in the tournament or in the NBA.</p>
<p>Lloyd's buyout reportedly dropped from $11 million to $9 million Thursday. The financial picture at Chapel Hill is complex, the Tar Heels still owe Davis more than $5 million, have committed tens of millions to coach Bill Belichick and the football program, and would face significant salary demands from any top coaching hire. Lloyd currently earns $5.3 million annually, while the five highest-paid college coaches in the country make between $6 million and $9 million.</p>
<p>In just his fifth season leading Arizona, Lloyd has guided the Wildcats to their first Final Four appearance since 1997. The program reached college basketball's premier event with a deep eight-man rotation featuring Big 12 Player of the Year Jaden Bradley and projected first-round picks Koa Peat and Brayden Burries, both freshmen. Arizona and Michigan rank first and second respectively in KenPom's national rankings.</p>
<p>Lloyd remained consistent when pressed further on the subject.</p>
<p>"Listen, I've got my full focus on this team. Nothing is distracting me," he said. "And that's just how I decided to approach it."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 22:17:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284865/Arizonas-Tommy-Lloyd-Im-Not-A-Multitasker-Amid-North-Carolina-Head-Coach-Rumors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lloyd_Tommy_az_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Duren, Pistons Both Say They Want Long-Term Deal In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284864/Jalen-Duren-Pistons-Both-Say-They-Want-Long-Term-Deal-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Duren and the Detroit Pistons both say they want a long-term partnership despite failing to reach a contract extension by the deadline last October, making the 22-year-old center a restricted free agent this summer. Both sides insist the impasse was purely a business matter.</p>
<p>"I mean, it was what it was," Duren said. "I think it's tough because there is a business aspect to it. Like I said, I would love to be a Piston for life. It was nothing personal. Nothing I took personal with the organization or anything. It was just business. They felt one way, I felt another. And at that moment we couldn't come to an agreement."</p>
<p>General manager Trajan Langdon echoed that sentiment from the organization's side.</p>
<p>"We want him to be a Piston for life, too," Langdon said. "It had nothing to do with what he had or hadn't done."</p>
<p>The contract situation looms over what has otherwise been a breakthrough season for Duren. He made his first All-Star team in February and is posting career highs across the board, averaging 19.5 points on 64.7 percent shooting and nearly 11 rebounds per game. He ranks among the frontrunners for the league's Most Improved Player award.</p>
<p>Duren has also shouldered the offensive load during Cade Cunningham's eight-game absence due to a collapsed lung, averaging 23.4 points, 10.5 rebounds and 1.8 assists while guiding Detroit to a 6-2 record. The Pistons clinched the Central Division title Tuesday and sit atop the Eastern Conference with six games remaining.</p>
<p>Cunningham and Duren have built one of the league's most promising young duos, traveling together to Italy last summer and deepening a bond that traces back to their AAU and USA Basketball days. Duren has drawn comparisons to the Julian Edelman-Tom Brady dynamic in terms of his deliberate investment in building chemistry with his star teammate.</p>
<p>With the playoffs approaching as the East's top seed, Duren's focus remains on the present despite the unresolved contract situation.</p>
<p>"I just want this team to use its full potential," Duren said. "I want to come out of this season thinking, 'Yeah. We did everything that we wanted to do.'"</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 02 Apr 2026 22:10:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284864/Jalen-Duren-Pistons-Both-Say-They-Want-Long-Term-Deal-In-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Duren_Jalen_det_251210.jpg" />
   </item>

      <description><p>Jock Landale sustained a right ankle injury during the fourth quarter of Wednesday night's 130-101 win by the Atlanta Hawks at the Orlando Magic. Landale has been diagnosed with a right high ankle sprain and will be re-evaluated in approximately two weeks.</p>
